title: Structured Retrieval, Knowledge Graphs, GraphRAG, and Multi-Hop Evidence · Program Brief: Rag Multihop Path Evidence
summary: A team converts every document into a generated graph, loses edge provenance, uses community summaries as facts, and never compares the result with SQL or baseline retrieval. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the multihop path evidence increment in provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever. Learners progress t…
objectives: Plan bounded multi-hop retrieval, preserve path and source evidence, detect cycles and missing links, and compare against a simpler baseline.
Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-structured-graph-rag-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-structured-graph-rag-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_structured_graph_rag_4(entities: int = 12, relations: int = 18, hops: int = 2, provenance_edges: int = 18, schema_valid: bool = True, baseline_beaten: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:
        return False, 0, "graph-shape"
    if hops < 1 or hops > 4:
        return False, hops, "hop-budget"
    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"
    if not schema_valid:
        return False, relations, "structured-contract"
    if not baseline_beaten:
        return False, relations, "complexity-not-earned"
    assert provenance_edges >= relations
    return True, hops, "grounded-multihop"

# Evidence: rag-multihop-path-evidence-rugqre
# Competency: rag-multihop-path-evidence.
# Case 4101: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects.

def evidence_rag_multihop_path_evidence_rugqre(entities: int = 12, relations: int = 18, hops: int = 2, provenance_edges: int = 18, schema_valid: bool = True, baseline_beaten: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_rugqre = "rugqre-erqgur-4101"
    assert evidence_variant_rugqre
    evidence_axes_rugqre = (
        "corpusrugqre",
        "queryerqgur",
        "tenant4101",
        "indexrugqre4101",
        "prompterqgur4101",
        "evaluation4101rugqre",

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_rugqre)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:

    if hops < 1 or hops > 4:

    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"

        return False, relations, "structured-contract"
    if not baseline_beaten:
        return False, relations, "complexity-not-earned"
    assert provenance_edges >= relations
    return True, hops, "grounded-multihop"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Multihop Path Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6197 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan bounded multi-hop retrieval, preserve path and source evidence, detect cycles and missing links, and compare against a simpler baseline.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Multihop Path Evidence model · Theory Rag Multihop Path Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Multihop Path Evidence model · Theory Rag Multihop Path Evidence, keep Rag Multihop Path Evidence connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan bounded multi-hop retrieval, preserve path and source evidence, detect cycles and missing links, and compare against a simpler baseline.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Multihop Path Evidence model · Theory Rag Multihop Path Evidence, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Multihop Path Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Multihop Path Evidence model · Theory Rag Multihop Path Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6197 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan bounded multi-hop retrieval, preserve path and source evidence, detect cycles and missing links, and compare against a simpler baseline.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Multihop Path Evidence model · Theory Rag Multihop Path Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: A connected graph path proves the multi-hop conclusion is true.
